rationale: The citation is used for more than background: the paper explicitly summarizes Hutchinson et al. (2010) as showing that line graphs increase reliance on period-to-period changes, and uses that point in its literature map and theory development. It then builds hypotheses about line vs. bar graphs and transition utilization using this prior finding as a conceptual stepping stone. In the discussion, the authors also state they are extending prior work on visual representations and data-based inferences, again naming Hutchinson et al. (2010).
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Hutchinson et al. (2010)'s result that graph format can increase use of local changes in data, and extends that logic to animated displays and risk inference. It proposes and tests a new salience-to-utilization process and boundary conditions in financial decision contexts.
